evidence_quotes.quote: Lack of protection against these unrestricted decisions has already cost countless consumers access to their rightfully purchased products.

paragraphs.text: Good day, especially during times like these where digital media and -products are omnipresent I find it important to protect our rights of truthful advertising and strengthen protection against predatory business practices. Something that I haven't seen on the scope of this act yet though is the protection of ownership of digital games in general, and online-access games in specific, leaving us at the mercy of a company's assessment whether it is worthwhile to keep a game running or not. Lack of protection against these unrestricted decisions has already cost countless consumers access to their rightfully purchased products. Is there any chance this aspect could also be added to the Digital Fairness Act? Yours sincerely
